creator: neldredge@math.ucsd.edu
text:
On Tue, 23 Oct 2007, Nate Eldredge wrote:

> On Mon, 22 Oct 2007, Nate Eldredge wrote:
>
>> Hi folks,
>> 
>> I think I have encountered this bug.
>
> Rats, I can't reproduce it anymore.  Now trying to read the affected section 
> of the disk just hangs the system.  When on the console, the screen scrolls 
> by one line and the block cursor disappears, but no message is printed. 
> Numlock and Ctrl-Alt-Esc don't work, so it seems the machine is totally dead.

Well, that's evidently a separate bug, but I found another way to
reproduce the current one.  I added a printf to check when dev->devclass
is NULL in dev_attach.  When I do "atacontrol reinit ata2" my printf
triggers.  Oddly, the device with null devclass is ad6, which is actually
attached to ata3.  ata2 has the broken disk which is ad4.

Anyway, the device in question was created by ata_add_child
(dev/ata/ata-all.c:555).  It calls device_add_child with the second
argument of NULL, which causes the created device to have a NULL devclass.

At this point I don't know what the right fix is.  It looks like it is
legal for a device to have no devclass.  If so, then maybe
device_sysctl_init should not call devclass_sysctl_init when devclass is
NULL.

-- 
Nate Eldredge
neldredge@math.ucsd.edu
